message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker — close bd-kk0w0, advance bd-vb2b7 to in_progress

Two issue status changes in the beads JSONL tracker:

- bd-kk0w0 ("wire ProgramConfig.runtime_lane into direct backend selection"):
  Closed as completed. The runtime lane now resolves its preferred task
  executor backend automatically, so ProgramConfig.runtime_lane feeds
  directly into EffectQueueConfig without manual wiring by callers.

- bd-vb2b7 ("optimize doctor_frankentui orchestration and evidence economy"):
  Moved from open to in_progress. Active work is underway to refactor
  capture subprocess management, suite orchestration, and state-effect
  detection accuracy — all aimed at reducing redundant work while
  preserving evidence quality and replayability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-kkn1","title":"Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests","description":"# Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests

## Background
Part of Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction (bd-2d2v). Tests ensure clipboard works across terminals.

## Unit Tests
- osc52_encoding_correct: OSC 52 sequence properly base64 encoded
- fallback_to_system_clipboard: When OSC 52 fails, use pbcopy/xclip/wl-copy
- read_clipboard_works: Can read back what was written
- large_content_handled: Multi-KB content works correctly
- binary_content_base64_encoded: Non-text content encoded properly

## Integration Tests
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_iterm: Full cycle works in iTerm2
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_wezterm: Full cycle works in WezTerm
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_kitty: Full cycle works in Kitty
- fallback_works_on_dumb_terminal: System clipboard used when OSC 52 unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass on major terminal emulators
- [ ] Graceful fallback when clipboard not available","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:33:38.691212422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.313064402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.312998518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kkn1","depends_on_id":"bd-2d2v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-l6yba","title":"epic(graveyard-§15.1): S3-FIFO Cache for Layout, Width, and Style Caches","description":"S3-FIFO (Yang 2023 SOSP) replaces LRU/TinyLFU. EV=80.0 (highest). 3 FIFO queues (small/main/ghost), atomic bit access, no list reordering. Apply to width_cache, layout/cache, stylesheet, measure_cache. Shadow-run vs W-TinyLFU. Fallback if hit-rate posterior drops. Isomorphism: identical query results regardless of eviction. Risk: constants (S16.6), mitigated by benchmark gate.

**User Value:** Delivers measurably lower render latency and memory usage for apps with large text buffers, complex layouts, or rapid terminal resizing.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- S3-FIFO replaces W-TinyLFU in width_cache with equivalent or better hit rate
- Layout memoization switches from LRU with no regression
- Benchmark shows p99 latency reduction on 100K-cell screens
- Memory footprint within 10% of current cache
- All existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:46:27.811271746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:56.158230858Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:56.158211782Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: l6yba.1 (S3Fifo impl), l6yba.2 (width_cache), l6yba.3 (layout cache), l6yba.4 (benchmarks), l6yba.5 (property tests). Commits: bf9ab11a, da42b9fb, ab704cc8","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","graveyard","performance","ring2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l6yba","depends_on_id":"bd-3jlw5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l6yba","depends_on_id":"bd-nv2fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-v6y","title":"Implement ASCII width fast-path optimization","description":"# Implement ASCII Width Fast-Path Optimization

## Background
The plan emphasizes that ASCII must dominate common workloads:
- Section 0.12: "ASCII fast-path must remain allocation-free and dominate common workloads"
- Section 4.3: ASCII width is a major optimization opportunity

Most harness/log output is ASCII. For ASCII:
- display width == byte length
- no grapheme segmentation needed
- no Unicode tables needed

## Scope
- Provide a fast, allocation-free `ascii_width` / `display_width` helper.
- Integrate it into text measurement / wrapping / truncation hot paths.

## Design
### Core API
```rust
/// Returns Some(width) if s is pure ASCII; otherwise None.
#[inline]
pub fn ascii_width(s: &str) -> Option<usize> {
    let bytes = s.as_bytes();
    if bytes.iter().all(|&b| b < 0x80) {
        Some(bytes.len())
    } else {
        None
    }
}

#[inline]
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize {
    ascii_width(s).unwrap_or_else(|| unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(s))
}
```

### Performance notes
- ASCII detection is O(n) but extremely cheap (tight loop over bytes).
- Optional micro-optimizations (still safe): use `memchr` or portable SIMD via `std::simd` if available.
- **Repo policy: unsafe is forbidden**, so any SIMD path must be safe or it is out of scope.

## Integration points
- Segment/Text measurement
- Wrapping/truncation (byte-fast for ASCII)
- Viewport/log viewer rendering

## Tests
- Pure ASCII returns correct width
- Mixed ASCII/Unicode returns None from `ascii_width`
- Empty string returns Some(0)
- Integration tests: wrapping/truncation correctness unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ascii_width` and `display_width` exist.
- [ ] ASCII path is allocation-free.
- [ ] Non-ASCII path matches `unicode_width`.
- [ ] Integrated into the ftui-text measurement code paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:52:10.389324927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790245126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790179993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:34Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-v857w","title":"Create consolidated Getting Started guide for library consumers","description":"Issue #10: Consumer documentation exists but is scattered and has API drift. The agent-harness.md tutorial (440 lines) is comprehensive but uses outdated API patterns (App::inline() instead of actual App::new().screen_mode()). README is 1600+ lines, burying the getting-started path. New users cannot find a clear adoption path.

APPROACH: Fix existing docs first, then create minimal landing page. Do NOT duplicate tutorial content.

Implementation:

Phase 1 - Fix API Drift in Existing Docs:
1. Audit docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md code examples against actual API:
   - Verify App::inline(), App::fullscreen() calls match actual constructor patterns
   - Verify import paths match current crate structure
   - Fix any examples that use deprecated Program::new() (README lines 92-138)
   - Ensure all code blocks compile against current crates
2. Update README "Minimal API Example" section (lines 92-138) to use current API
3. Verify all 5 examples in crates/ftui-harness/examples/ compile and run:
   - minimal.rs (77 lines), streaming.rs (162 lines), counter.rs (95 lines), layout.rs, modal.rs

Phase 2 - Add CI Gate for Doc Examples:
4. Add to .github/workflows/ci.yml docs job:
   - name: Test doc examples
     run: cargo test --doc --workspace
   This catches future API drift in rustdoc examples automatically.

5. Add example compilation check:
   - name: Build examples
     run: cargo build --examples -p ftui-harness
   Ensures all 5 examples compile on every CI run.

Phase 3 - Create Minimal Getting Started Landing Page:
6. Create docs/getting-started.md (target: < 200 lines, NOT a tutorial):
   - "Start Here" header with 1-paragraph project summary
   - Dependency setup: which crates to add to Cargo.toml
     - Published on crates.io: ftui-core, ftui-layout, ftui-i18n
     - Not yet published (use git dep): ftui-harness, ftui-widgets, ftui-extras, etc.
   - Decision tree:
     - "I want an inline CLI UI" -> minimal.rs example + agent-harness.md Part 1
     - "I want full-screen TUI" -> agent-harness.md Part 5 (Inline vs Alt-Screen)
     - "I want to capture subprocess output" -> agent-harness.md Part 6 (PTY)
   - Examples index table:
     | Example | Use Case | Size | Run Command |
     |---------|----------|------|-------------|
     | minimal.rs | Inline UI with log viewer | 77 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example minimal |
     | streaming.rs | High-volume log output | 162 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example streaming |
     | counter.rs | State management | 95 LOC | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example counter |
     | layout.rs | Flex/grid composition | - | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example layout |
     | modal.rs | Modal dialogs | - | cargo run -p ftui-harness --example modal |
   - WIP status: clearly state API stability expectations
   - Link to agent-harness.md tutorial for hands-on walkthrough
   - Link to docs/concepts/screen-modes.md for architecture understanding

7. Add prominent link in README.md after TL;DR section (around line 20):
   > **New here?** Start with [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md) for dependency setup and a guided path to your first FrankenTUI app.

TESTS:

Doc Tests (CI-enforced):
- cargo test --doc --workspace: all rustdoc examples compile and pass
- cargo build --examples -p ftui-harness: all 5 examples compile
- cargo run -p ftui-harness --example minimal -- --help: minimal example runs without panic

Content Validation (manual checklist):
- [ ] All code blocks in agent-harness.md match actual API
- [ ] All code blocks in getting-started.md match actual API
- [ ] README minimal example compiles
- [ ] Decision tree links resolve to correct docs
- [ ] Published crate versions match Cargo.toml

Link Validation:
- [ ] All relative links in getting-started.md resolve
- [ ] README -> getting-started.md link works
- [ ] getting-started.md -> agent-harness.md link works
- [ ] getting-started.md -> examples link works

-{"id":"bd-vb2b7","title":"feature(doctor-perf): optimize doctor_frankentui orchestration and evidence economy","description":"Background:\ndoctor_frankentui is both a performance consumer and a confidence engine. It launches subprocesses, captures evidence, writes artifacts, and helps operators understand failures. Performance work here must therefore reduce redundant work without weakening evidence quality or replayability.\n\nGoal:\nProfile and optimize the most expensive doctor workflows while preserving or improving the quality of summaries, manifests, replay helpers, and failure triage artifacts.","design":"Feature scope:\n- Profile workflow-level cost centers across capture, replay, report, and failure handling.\n- Remove duplicate subprocess, artifact, and comparison work.\n- Tighten artifact contracts so evidence stays rich but no longer bloated or repetitive.\n- Validate with soak, shadow, and deterministic replay suites.\n\nGuiding principle:\nThe output of doctor work must stay useful to humans during failure, not merely become cheaper to compute.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Optimization decisions are grounded in workflow-level evidence rather than isolated function timings.
2. Artifact quality, replayability, and operator clarity remain first-class acceptance criteria.
3. The resulting doctor lane improves both throughput and the usability of failure evidence.
4. The lane is incomplete without focused unit/integration coverage, scripted failure-path and soak E2E runs, and detailed logging/manifests that explain every mismatch or fallback.","notes":"Doctor work is easy to accidentally optimize in the wrong direction by deleting valuable evidence or making failure modes less intelligible. The bead text therefore makes evidence economy, not artifact austerity, the target.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:46:22.840986034Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T19:05:03.113379361Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","diagnostics","doctor_frankentui","extreme-software-optimization","ftui-performance-control-plane","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-mn4a0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:24.291330399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-qbbkv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:21.280349810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-tsgb4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:27.297874255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vb2b7","depends_on_id":"bd-zmcvz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:58:30.303349902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-vcq4","title":"Advanced Text Editor: Undo history panel + shortcuts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:39.337226162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:44.403503036Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T07:41:44.403483710Z","close_reason":"Implemented undo/redo history panel, shortcuts, status indicator, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-vins","title":"Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes","description":"## Background
The typewriter effect is limited to left-to-right reveal. A comprehensive reveal system enables dramatic text appearances for titles, announcements, and onboarding.

## Implementation

Add RevealMode enum (used as parameter, not nested in TextEffect):
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub enum RevealMode {
    #[default]
    LeftToRight,    // Classic typewriter
    RightToLeft,    // Reverse typewriter  
    CenterOut,      // Expand from middle
    EdgesIn,        // Converge to center
    Random,         // Random order (deterministic with seed)
    ByWord,         // Word at a time
    ByLine,         // Line at a time (for multi-line)
}
```

Add TextEffect variant (consistent structure with others):
```rust
TextEffect::Reveal {
    mode: RevealMode,
    progress: f64,      // 0.0 = hidden, 1.0 = fully revealed
    seed: u64,          // For Random mode determinism
}

// Separate variant for gradient mask (different parameters)
TextEffect::RevealMask {
    angle: f64,         // Sweep angle in degrees (0 = left-to-right)
    progress: f64,      // Mask position 0.0-1.0
    softness: f64,      // Edge softness 0.0 (hard) to 1.0 (gradient)
}
```

### Visibility Calculation by Mode:

| Mode | Visible When |
|------|--------------|
| LeftToRight | char_idx < progress * total_chars |
| RightToLeft | char_idx >= (1-progress) * total_chars |
| CenterOut | distance_from_center < progress * max_distance |
| EdgesIn | distance_from_edge < progress * max_distance |
| Random | hash(seed, idx) < progress * total_chars |
| ByWord | word_idx < progress * total_words |
| ByLine | line_idx < progress * total_lines |
| RevealMask | rotated_position > threshold (with softness fade) |

### Usage:
```rust
// Simple left-to-right reveal
StyledText::new("Hello World")
    .effect(TextEffect::Reveal { 
        mode: RevealMode::LeftToRight, 
        progress: 0.5,
        seed: 0,
    })

// Dramatic gradient mask sweep
StyledText::new("WELCOME")
    .effect(TextEffect::RevealMask {
        angle: 45.0,
        progress: 0.5,
        softness: 0.3,
    })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_reveal_ltr_first_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows first half
- test_reveal_rtl_last_chars_first: progress=0.5 shows last half
- test_reveal_center_out_middle_first: Center chars visible first
- test_reveal_edges_in_edges_first: Edge chars visible first
- test_reveal_random_deterministic: Same seed = same reveal order
- test_reveal_random_all_chars_reveal: progress=1 shows all
- test_reveal_by_word_whole_words: Partial progress shows complete words
- test_reveal_by_line_whole_lines: Lines reveal atomically
- test_reveal_mask_angle_0: Left-to-right sweep
- test_reveal_mask_angle_90: Top-to-bottom sweep  
- test_reveal_mask_softness_0: Hard edge (binary visible/hidden)
- test_reveal_mask_softness_1: Gradient fade across chars

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot CenterOut at progress 0.25, 0.5, 0.75
- Snapshot RevealMask at angle=45, softness=0.3

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Character Reveal Modes
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-vmuax","title":"E2E: Doom/Quake braille visual effects (input + render hashes)","description":"Scope: E2E script for DoomE1M1 and QuakeE1M1 braille renderers. Navigate to Visual Effects, select Doom and Quake, simulate inputs (WASD, mouse look, fire), and capture per-frame hashes.
